title: facility — helper functions
description: Ergonomic helpers for accessing the active facility from the request context, resolving pricing, checking availability, and computing pass credits. All helpers are thin wrappers around bound services — never business logic.

helpers.purpose: Returns the resolved Facility for the current request (set by facility.resolve_active middleware). Null when no facility context.
helpers.example:
$facility = active_facility();
if ($facility === null) throw new NoActiveFacilityException();

helpers.purpose: Returns the resolved Facility.id or null. Convenience for creators that need the ID without loading the model.

Returns the resolved booking price in cents. Consults facility.pricing_json + peak_hours + attendees_count via PricingCalculator. Delegated to the bound service so tenants swapping the calculator swap this too.
Check whether a facility is available for a given window (respects availability_json + blackout_dates but NOT the overlap check). Cheap read-only check for UI-side gating.
Returns the pass that would cover a booking-create for this user + facility + date, or null. Used by the SDUI booking flow to render 'pay with pass' vs 'pay full' branches.
Materialises a unique receipt number in the tenant's yearly sequence. Consumed by DayPassObserver on create.

notes: Helpers deliberately delegate — testing swaps the bound service to a stub, and every helper follows.
No helper directly touches the DB — everything routes through repositories or services. Keeps the boundary clean.
